title: Adam Watene
wikitext:
{{Short description|Cook Islands rugby league footballer}} {{Use British English|date=March 2014}} {{Use dmy dates|date=April 2026}} {{Infobox rugby league biography |name = Adam Watene |fullname = |image = Adam Watene.jpg |image_size = 300px |position = {{rlp|PR}} |birth_date = Adam Cook
{{birth date|1977|10|07|df=y}} |birth_place = New Zealand |death_date = {{death date and age|2008|10|13|1977|7|20|df=y}} |death_place = [[Wakefield]], West Yorkshire, England |height = {{convert|6|ft|2|in|m|abbr=on}} |weight = {{convert|17|st|0|lb|kg|abbr=on}} |club1 = [[Castleford Tigers]] |year1start = 2005 |year1end = |appearances1 = 28 |tries1 = 6 |goals1 = 0 |fieldgoals1 = 0 |points1 = 24 |club2 = [[Bradford Bulls]] |year2start = 2006 |year2end = |appearances2 = 4 |tries2 = 0 |goals2 = 0 |fieldgoals2 = 0 |points2 = 0 |club3 = {{nowrap|[[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]]}} |year3start = 2006 |year3end = 08 |appearances3 = 58 |tries3 = 6 |goals3 = 0 |fieldgoals3 = 0 |points3 = 24 |teamA = [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]] |yearAstart = 2000 |yearAend = 06 |appearancesA = 3 |triesA = 0 |goalsA = 0 |fieldgoalsA = 0 |pointsA = 0 |updated = 4 May 2008 |source = [http://www.rugbyleagueproject.com/players/Adam_Watene.html Rugby League Project] | module = {{infobox person | embed = yes | father = | mother = | relatives = [[Frank Watene]] (brother) | education = }} }} '''Adam Watene''' (7 October 1977 – 13 October 2008), known as '''Adam Cook''' early in his career, also known by the [[nickname]]s of '''"[[Morlock]]"''', '''"Monster"''', and '''"The Gentle Giant"''', was a [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 2000s. He played at club level in Australia for the [[Wynnum Manly Seagulls]], [[Burleigh Bears]], and in England for the [[Castleford Tigers]], the [[Bradford Bulls]] and the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] as a {{rlp|pr}}.{{cite web|last1=Webeck|first1=Tony|title=Pain behind premiership push|url=http://www.nrl.com/pain-behind-premiership-push/tabid/10874/newsid/82142/default.aspx|website=NRL.com|accessdate=26 November 2017|date=27 September 2014}} ==Background== Watene was born in [[New Zealand]]. He was of [[Māori people|Maori]], and Cook Islander descent.{{cite web |url=https://www.nzherald.co.nz/nz/news/article.cfm?c_id=1&objectid=10538142 |title=Tragic end to league star's fairy-tale life |publisher=New Zealand Herald |date=17 October 2008 |accessdate=11 August 2020}} He grew up with fellow rugby league footballer, [[Adam Wright (rugby league)|Adam Wright]]. Adam was the younger brother of [[rugby league]] footballer, [[Frank Watene]]. ==Career== As Adam Cook he won caps for [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]] in the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]].{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/Adam-Cook/summary.html|title=Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org|publisher=rugbyleagueproject.org|date=2009-12-31|accessdate=2010-01-01}}[http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/world_cup_2000/group_2/992546.stm Wales v Cook Islands preview] ''bbc.co.uk'', 27 October 2000 Watene played for [[Wynnum Manly Seagulls]] and [[Burleigh Bears]] in the [[Queensland Cup]] in Australia before moving to the UK in 2005 to join the [[Castleford Tigers]] in [[RFL Championship|National League One]]. In his last season with the Burleigh Bears he won the [[2004 Queensland Cup season|2004 Queensland Cup]]. His move to Castleford was in doubt because of visa issues which led to him being chased by the [[Newcastle Thunder|Gateshead Thunder]] but eventually he joined Castleford.[http://www.thefreelibrary.com/Cook+is+off+the+menu+Thunder.-a0127351046 Cook is off the Menu - Thunder] ''Evening Chronicle'', 20 January 2005 In 2005 Watene helped the Castleford Tigers gain promotion to [[Super League]] and played alongside his brother [[Frank Watene]].{{cite book|last1=Davis|first1=John|title=Tigers' Top Tens 1926-2015: Teamwork|date=17 May 2017|publisher=Lulu.com|isbn=9780244606152}} He was signed by [[Brian Noble (rugby league)|Brian Noble]] in 2006 for the [[Bradford Bulls]][http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/bradford/4378126.stm Bradford sign Tigers prop Watene] but only spent one year at the club before leaving for the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]].[http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/wakefield/4966732.stm Wakefield sign duo from Bradford] In 2007 Watene had an outstanding season, despite missing part of the year due to injury and the death of his father. ==Death== On 13 October 2008 Wakefield announced that he died of a suspected heart attack at the age of 31 after collapsing during a weight training session in the gym.[https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/3191276/Adam-Watenes-death-from-heart-attack-stuns-Wakefield-Trinity-Wildcats-Rugby-League.html Adam Watene's death from heart attack stuns Wakefield Trinity Wildcats], David Burke, ''Daily Telegraph'', 13 October 2008{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=12049|title=Wildcats mourn death of Watene|publisher=Super League|date=13 October 2008|accessdate=13 October 2008|archiveurl= https://web.archive.org/web/20081016011428/http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=12049|archivedate= 16 October 2008 |url-status=dead}} [[Jason Demetriou (rugby league)|Jason Demetriou]] wore Watene's number 8 jersey in the season after Watene's death to honour his memory.{{cite web |url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/4307101/Wakefield-captain-Jason-Demetriou-to-honour-Adam-Watenes-memory.html |title=Wakefield captain Jason Demetriou to honour Adam Watene's memory |author=Michael Fisher |publisher=The Telegraph |date=21 January 2009 |accessdate=11 August 2020}} ==Personal life== Watene had a wife, Moana Watene, and two children.{{cite web|url=http://www.therhinos.co.uk/news/3514.php|publisher=Leeds Rhinos|title=Rugby 'Wags' Show Posh And Co The Way!|access-date=23 April 2011|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111126214645/http://therhinos.co.uk/news/3514.php|archive-date=26 November 2011|url-status=dead}} At the time of his death he held the ceremonial position of the consort of [[Wakefield]]'s deputy mayor, Heather Hudson.{{cite web|last1=Hadfield|first1=Dave|title=Wakefield in shock as Watene dies, aged 31|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/wakefield-in-shock-as-watene-dies-aged-31-960284.html |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081202144351/http://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/wakefield-in-shock-as-watene-dies-aged-31-960284.html |archive-date=2008-12-02 |url-access=limited |url-status=live|website=Independent|accessdate=26 November 2017}} ==Legacy== Two of Watene's former clubs in each hemisphere compete for specially commissioned trophies named in his honor. Since 2009 Castleford Tigers and Wakefield Trinity have chosen one of their fixtures to contest the Adam Watene trophy.{{cite web|last1=Arthur|first1=Bill|title=Humble in The Jungle|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/11072/4827464/humble-in-the-jungle|website=SkySports|accessdate=26 November 2017}}{{cite web|title=ADAM WATENE TROPHY|url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=5378|website=CastlefordTigers.com|accessdate=26 November 2017|language=English|date=10 April 2017}} And in the Southern Hemisphere Burleigh Bears and Wynnum Manly Seagulls play annually for the Adam Watene trophy.{{cite web|title=Bears And Seagulls Play For Adam Watene (Cook) Trophy|url=http://www.burleighbears.com.au/bears-and-seagulls-play-for-adam-watene-cook-trophy/|website=Burleigh Bears Leagues Club|accessdate=26 November 2017|archive-date=1 December 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171201030815/http://www.burleighbears.com.au/bears-and-seagulls-play-for-adam-watene-cook-trophy/|url-status=dead}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/adam-watene/summary.html Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org (as Adam Watene)] *[http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/adam-cook/summary.html Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org (as Adam Cook)] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20120216084315/http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=140 Profile at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20070927210339/http://www.wakefieldwildcats.co.uk/adam-watene-i164.html Wakefield Trinity Wildcats profile] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20090904201642/http://www.superleague.co.uk/profile.php?id=2380 Super League profile] {{Cook Islands squad 2000 Rugby League World Cup}} {{Burleigh Bears squad -
